notes.text: To expand a single bracket, multiply every term inside the bracket by the term outside. For example, $3(x + 2)$ becomes $3x + 6$.
Multiply the outside term by each term inside, one at a time.
Remember to multiply the second term too — a common mistake is to write $2x + 5$.

items: Multiply the outside term by the first term inside.
Multiply the outside term by the second term inside.
Combine the results, keeping the operator.
